headline: 2024 Floods Cost Kelantan RM4.3B but Mitigation Stalled
context: The 2024 monsoon floods caused RM4.3 billion in damages across Kelantan, while the RM1.8 billion flood mitigation project approved in 2017 remains only 34% complete.

cards.big: Federal government pledged RM1.8B for Kelantan flood mitigation in 2017.
cards.sub: After 7 years and 3 changes of government, only 34% of the project is complete.

cards.big: Annual flood damage exceeds RM2B; mitigation spending averages RM180M.
cards.sub: The cost-benefit ratio is 11:1 against inaction, yet disbursement remains delayed.

cards.big: DID redesigned the project 3 times due to political boundary changes.
cards.sub: Each redesign added 18 months. Technical scope changed to match constituency priorities.

cards.big: 78% of Kelantan flood victims are from B40 households.
cards.sub: Repeated displacement compounds poverty — average affected family loses 4.2 months of income per event.

cards.big: Flood mitigation is not engineering. It is a political commitment that keeps failing.
When annual damage is 11 times the annual spend, the problem is not resources but prioritisation. Floods are predictable; inaction is a choice.
